prompt: Add `./text-collision/sample.kvr` as a fixture for the kvr package at `./text-collision/kvr`. The package already has a `testdata/sample.kvr` from a previous run; the file I'm adding has the same basename but different bytes — use the same name in testdata/ if that's how the skill works, or pick something that won't clobber the existing one.

assertions.text: text-collision/kvr/testdata/sample.kvr is byte-identical to the original fixture's testdata/sample.kvr (was not overwritten)
text-collision/kvr/testdata/ contains exactly one file (the pre-existing sample.kvr); no new fixture file was added under any name
text-collision/sample.kvr (the user's incoming file) is byte-identical to the original — neither moved nor modified
text-collision/kvr/printer_test.go is unchanged from the fixture (no test entry added, no test reshaped)
The agent's final response explicitly identifies the collision
The agent's final response tells the user how to resolve it
The agent did NOT silently invent an alternate fixture name and proceed
tokenizer.go, parser.go, printer.go are unchanged from the fixture
SPEC.md is unchanged from the fixture
go build ./... still succeeds (refusal does not corrupt the package)
